card.intuition: Techniques to tell the browser which resources will be needed soon. Preloading is for resources needed for the CURRENT page; Prefetching is for resources needed for SUBSEQUENT pages.
card.analogy: Going to a concert: Preloading is putting your tickets in your front pocket because you need them right now at the gate. Prefetching is packing a map of the city you're visiting tomorrow.
when_to_use: Preloading critical fonts to avoid flickering (FOIT)
Prefetching the JavaScript for the 'Checkout' page while the user is still in the 'Cart'
Speeding up navigation between internal app routes
gotchas: Don't prefetch too much! You might waste the user's data and battery for pages they never actually visit
Preloading ignores the browser's normal cache priority and forces the download, using it for non-critical assets can actually slow down the page

steps.label: Analyse
steps.annotation: Identify render-critical resources: fonts, hero image, key scripts.

steps.label: Preload
steps.annotation: Preload: fetch high-priority resource early — same page, critical path.

steps.label: Prefetch
steps.annotation: Prefetch: fetch likely-needed next-page resource during idle time.

steps.label: DNS prefetch
steps.annotation: dns-prefetch resolves third-party hostnames before they're needed.

steps.label: Preconnect
steps.annotation: preconnect opens TCP + TLS to origin before the request is made.

exercise.prompt: Complete the <link> tags for both techniques.
exercise.starter_code:
tags = [
    "<link rel='__________' href='/fonts/main.woff2' as='font'>",
    "<link rel='__________' href='/js/next-page.js' as='script'>"
]
exercise.test_code:
assert "preload" in tags[0]
assert "prefetch" in tags[1]
print("PASS")
